event.summary: Claimed one ready iteration unit without expanding repository or path scope.

blockers.resume_when: Correct the failure and explicitly resume the unit.

acceptance.proof: The manifest contains the exact expected ordered current-safe accepted checker inventory and every named checker exists.
Focused tests prove deterministic ordering, duplicate/missing/path rejection, stop-on-first-failure, and successful complete dispatch without executing historical checkers.
check_all invokes only the dispatcher for focused current gates and CI invokes check_all; direct checkers remain byte-unchanged from LSLC-003G.
The complete manifest-driven owner gate passes without feature-lock or runtime implementation changes, new dependencies, or broader authority.

document.commit_policy: Commit proposal and lifecycle separately, then manifest/dispatcher/tests/routing/instructions, validation, acceptance, and no-force publication evidence.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route current aggregate validation through the manifest and preserve direct historical checkers.
Name the supported current aggregate entrypoint and manifest role.
Document current versus direct historical checker routing and fail-closed behavior.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run dispatcher negative tests and owner gate.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review project validation routing and public/private boundary; current guidance already requires owner-local gates and append-only evidence.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Reviewed installed skill and pinned project workflow.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review validation authority, deterministic negative cases, CI trust boundary, and evidence roles; current guidance supports the manifest/dispatcher split.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Reviewed installed skill and evidence/validation/recovery reference.

non_scope: Editing immutable historical check_lslc_* scripts or their evidence claims.

Pinned-toolchain Clippy policy/baseline, first-hop documentation compaction, runtime formats/chunks/channels/discovery breadth, adapters, devices, official-source intake, dependencies, unsafe/FFI, or Manifold integration.
Feature descriptors, feature lock, runtime implementation, activation, protocol bytes, errors, limits, cancellation, or cleanup.
document.objective: Replace the omission-prone hand-coded current focused-checker chain with one explicit ordered manifest and fail-closed dispatcher while retaining immutable direct checker behavior and routing the owner aggregate and CI through that accepted mechanism.
outputs: A versioned current-gates manifest names every current-safe accepted focused checker from LSLC-002Q through LSLC-003G in deterministic order.
A dependency-free dispatcher rejects malformed, duplicate, missing, out-of-tools, or failing checker entries and stops at first failure.
check_all.ps1 and CI route current focused validation through the manifest while direct check_lslc scripts remain unchanged.
